Kentucky Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Kentucky

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Kentucky. To work in Kentucky, apply for licensure by endorsement through Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists.

Endorsement fee
$50
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Not listed
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of supervised practice.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

  3. Submit the Application via eServices

    Create an account on the Kentucky DPL eServices portal, submit the online application, and pay the $50 application fee.

  4. Provide Documentation

    Upload or mail a copy of your current active CDR registration card, official transcripts, and proof of supervised practice.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$50
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$50
Renewal cycle
Annual (by Oct 31st) + 15 CE hours

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Card: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official College Transcripts: Must show your conferred master's degree. Can be sent securely via Clearinghouse to the board.
  • Supervised Practice Verification: Documentation showing completion of at least 1,000 hours.

Board Contact

Board
Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists
Department
Department of Professional Licensing (DPL)
Phone
(502) 892-4254
Email
diet@ky.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $50. Renewal cycle: Annual (by Oct 31st) + 15 CE hours.

Kentucky Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Kentucky dietitian license cost?

The application fee for initial licensure as a dietitian (or dual licensure as a dietitian/nutritionist) is $50.

When does the Kentucky license need to be renewed?

Kentucky dietitian licenses expire annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $50. There is a 60-day grace period (until Dec 31st) with late fees applied.

What are the continuing education requirements?

Licensees must complete at least 15 hours of approved continuing education every year prior to the October 31st renewal deadline.

Does Kentucky have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Kentucky. To work in Kentucky, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Kentucky?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
